summary: Minute-by-minute report: Ante Budimir’s second-half goal secures a vital victory for Croatia. Join Jeff Rueter for reaction
content: The result vaults Croatia up to third in the third-placed teams standing, with just one group left to play its second fixture. Group L standings 1) England (4 pts, +2 GD) 2) Ghana (4 pts, +1 GD) 3) Croatia (3 pts, -1 GD) 4) Panama (0 pts, -2 GD) – e A lovely scene on the pitch as Croatia’s players don special shirts in honor of Modrić’s 200th cap, saying “Infinite Legacy” underneath some nice art of him taking a kick. The former Ballon d’Or winner seems a bit uncomfortable with all the adulation, though that feels in-character. It wasn’t pretty, but it didn’t need to be. Croatia gets three sorely needed points, while Panama is out after a pair of hard-fought 1-0 defeats. 90+3 min After a lot of great work up the right, Amir Murillo takes the free kick but sends it over the bar. Two minutes left for Croatia to sweat this one out… 90+1 min One of Davis’ first involvements is wasteful, as he sends a free kick right to Livaković. That said, Rodríguez draws another dead ball in much closer proximity as Petar Sucić is shown a yellow card… 90 min Two changes for Panama (and I missed one earlier) Fajardo, Bárcenas, and Blackman off. Azarías Lodoño, Tomás Rodríguez, and Éric Davis in. Five minutes of stoppage time 89 min This scrappy affair is nearing its end as Panama struggles to breach the Croatian box. 85 min Some good back and forth forcing blocked shots from both sides, and Croatia is trying to resume its first-half possession routine to eat up some clock. Panama is meeting their moment though and not giving an inch, trying to find an equalizer to keep their tournament hopes alive. 81 min Last change for Croatia, and Luka Modrić’s 200th cap ends with a loud ovation. In comes Mario Pasalić, who is not related to Marco Pasalić as far as I can find. 79 min Another corner for Panama, this time opting to not play the short route after the last few have gone wasted. Rodríguez’s looper finds Carlos Harvey beyond the far post, but the Minnesota United midfielder fires his attempt into the side-netting. 77 min Panama continue to work up their right flank, but Murillo is finally seeming to feel a bit tired. One sub for Los Canaleros: Jiovany Ramos off, Cecilio Waterman on to add some attacking threat. He scored a late winner against the US in the Concacaf Nations League last March – does he have more dramatics in tow today? Reader Billy is strolling down memory lane as Mateo Kovacić takes his seat on the bench… I remember when Kovacić was the future of Croatia AFTER Modrić and Rakitic. Now it looks like him, Modrić and Perisić are on their way out together. Time truly is a flat circle. 72 min More subs for Croatia as we enter the final quarter… Marco Pasalic and Mateo Kovacic off Petar and Luka Sučić in. They’re cousins. Reader Tom just wants it to make sense… So: there are eight third-placed teams going in the knockouts. There are twelve first-placed teams. It appears, from looking at the pre-tourney brackets, that eight groups were selected to have the group winner face a third-place team, while the winners of the other four group winners drew a second-place team. I haven’t seen a good explanation for this. Is there one? A great question that I wish I could answer. Best theory I’ve heard is that it was randomly determined, though that doesn’t seem like a great method to give two-thirds of group winners a leg up… 68 min PANAMA PUSHING FOR AN EQUALIZER! Cristian Martínez makes a nifty slide to keep the ball in play to kick off a sequence of in-box passes and attempts that are largely blocked by Croatia. Eventually Livaković is called into action for a save, while the ensuing corner forces the goalkeeper to make another stop. And right on cue, the game grinds to a halt so they can whet their whistles. Ughhhhhhhhh… 65 min For a team that has really struggled to turn crosses into viable scoring chances, it’s baffling that Panama continue to lean heavily on looping balls from wide areas. Credit to Croatia for gumming up the central channels and forcing Panama wide – it’s working a charm. 61 min A yellow card shown to Bárcenas after dropping a shoulder into the side of Luka Modrić. 57 min NEARLY ANOTHER! Croatia had an unimpeded run up the gut available as Panama commits numbers, with Modrić threading a ball into Pasalić’s stride. The Orlando City man tried to get Mosquera to commit, but the Panama goalkeeper stood his ground long enough to get a hand on a late looping chip by Pasalić. The ball again went into Pasalić’s stride, but his shot near the endline is skied into the seats. 54 min At long last, and in spite of some wasteful build-up, Croatia has found a breakthrough. Marco Pasalić plays a silky through ball behind his back along the line into Stanišić’s stride, and the Bayern defender crosses the ball in front of the goalkeeper to the far post. Budimir ran on with perfect timing, slamming the ball into the net with his torso to reward Zlatko Dalić for his halftime change. As it stands, Panama is 35 minutes (and stoppage) away from elimination. Finally! Someone got on the end of a cross! 52 min For a team that’s struggling to get targets in the box during the run of play, very curious to see Panama play their corner kick short. Nothing comes of it, as they dawdled too long to find a better pathway into the box before Croatia could clear. 52 min Loose passes galore for both teams, but Panama able to earn a corner kick after another speculative cross by Martínez… 49 min Tense moment for Croatia as Dominik Livaković stays on the ground after his foot is stomped launching a ball upfield. Foul wasn’t called, and the goalkeeper is quick to get up after the referee pauses play. 46 min Two changes for Croatia as play resumes to fix a couple problem areas from the first half. Josko Gvardiol gets a half-time hook as Amir Murillo had his way with their flank, while Petar Musa exits after an ineffective shift leading the line following his goalscoring World Cup debut against England. Ante Budimir and Andrej Kr
chineseTitle: 巴拿马 0-1 克罗地亚：2026 年世界杯 – 直播
chineseSummary: 详细报道：下半场安特·布迪米尔的进球为克罗地亚赢得了一场至关重要的胜利。加入杰夫·鲁特 (Jeff Rueter) 的反应
chineseContent: 这一结果使克罗地亚队在排名第三的球队中上升到第三位，只剩下一个小组进行第二场比赛。 L 组积分榜 1) 英格兰 (4 分，+2 GD) 2) 加纳 (4 分，+1 GD) 3) 克罗地亚 (3 分，-1 GD) 4) 巴拿马 (0 分，-2 GD) – e 球场上的一幕很可爱，克罗地亚球员穿着特殊的球衣纪念莫德里奇的第 200 场比赛，在他踢球的漂亮艺术下面写着“无限的遗产”。这位前金球奖得主似乎对所有的奉承感到有点不舒服，尽管这感觉很符合他的性格。它并不漂亮，但也没有必要。克罗地亚获得了急需的三分，而巴拿马则在两场苦战后以 1-0 失利而出局。 90+3 分钟 在右路做了很多出色的工作后，阿米尔·穆里略主罚任意球，但皮球高出横梁。还剩两分钟，克罗地亚队就将这场比赛淘汰了……90+1 分钟，戴维斯的第一次出场是浪费的，他将任意球传给了利瓦科维奇。也就是说，罗德里格斯在更接近的地方又打出了一个死球，佩塔尔·苏西奇被出示了一张黄牌……第 90 分钟，巴拿马队两次换人（我之前错过了一次）法哈多、巴尔塞纳斯和布莱克曼下场。阿扎里亚斯·洛多尼奥、托马斯·罗德里格斯和埃里克·戴维斯上场。 补时5分钟 89 分钟 这场斗志旺盛的比赛即将结束，巴拿马队努力突破克罗地亚禁区。第 85 分钟，双方的几次良好的来回迫使双方射门被封堵，克罗地亚正试图恢复上半场的控球常规，以消耗一些时间。不过，巴拿马队正在迎接他们的时刻，并没有让步，试图找到扳平比分的机会，以保持他们的锦标赛希望。 81 分钟，克罗地亚队最后一次换人，卢卡·莫德里奇第 200 次代表国家队出场，全场响起热烈的掌声。马里奥·帕萨利奇 (Mario Pasalić) 登场，据我所知，他与马可·帕萨利奇 (Marco Pasalić) 没有亲戚关系。 79分钟 又一个角球
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
